$description: Typographic conventions, special characters, and script-specific rules. This file captures what font files alone cannot express: how text is formatted, what symbols are used, and how different scripts interact in the same interface. The typographic features here are alaṅkāra — ornament that is simultaneously structure. A drop capital is beautiful and marks a chapter opening. A swelled rule is elegant and marks a scene boundary. Every ornamental element must be load-bearing. See aesthetic-theory.language.json for the theoretical framework.

characters.$description: Named Unicode characters used in the design language. Every character here has a specific semantic role — they are not decorative.

em-dash.role: Citation separator. Always followed by non-breaking space (U+00A0). Pattern: — Author Name

non-breaking-space.role: Prevents line break between em-dash and author name in citations.

ellipsis.role: Indicates omitted text in truncated passages. Single character, not three periods.

thin-space.role: Between number and unit, between initials. Narrower than regular space.

quotation-marks.$description: Per-language quotation mark conventions. Use the correct marks for each language — never straight quotes in published text.

primary.name: Guillemets (angle quotes) — standard in Spanish publishing

secondary.name: Double quotation marks for nested quotes

es.note: Some Latin American publishers use English-style double quotes as primary. SRF Spanish publications should be checked.

primary.name: Double quotation marks — standard in Hindi publishing

secondary.name: Single quotation marks for nested quotes

ornamental.$description: Breathing marks between movements of thought. The visual kumbhaka. Ordered by richness; motif system preferred, Unicode characters are text-only fallbacks (text-only mode, no-CSS, email).

lotus.implementation: Motif system:
lotus.color-voice: sacred (gold) by default; crimson under data-publication
lotus.note: The canonical lotus is a 33x23 two-path SVG — the simplest botanical glyph. For richer dividers, use the lotus-07/08/10 banner glyphs via the motif system.

fleuron.role: Text-only fallback. Rotated floral heart. Used when motif system is unavailable (plain text, email).

asterism.role: Minimal text-only fallback. Three dots in triangle. Last resort when neither motif nor fleuron is appropriate.

om.role: Om/Aum symbol. Used in Sanskrit/Hindi context only. Never as decoration.

sri.role: Shri prefix. Used before guru names in Hindi context. Written in Devanagari, not transliterated.

typographic-features.$description: CSS typographic features applied to the reading experience. These are the invisible details that make text feel professionally set.

drop-capitals.$description: Large initial letter at the start of each chapter. Merriweather 700 weight, 3.5em, floated inline-start. Creates a classic book-opening feel.

latin.color: theme-text (same as body — not gold, not decorative)

devanagari.rationale: Devanagari shirorekha conflicts with drop-capital spacing. Hindi literary tradition does not use drop capitals. ADR-080.

small-caps.$description: font-variant: small-caps with letter-spacing: 0.05em. Used for chapter labels ('CHAPTER I'), proper noun emphasis in editorial context.
small-caps.usage: Chapter labels, section titles in some contexts

hanging-punctuation.$description: hanging-punctuation: first allow-end. Opens quotes and punctuation extend into the margin for optical alignment. Progressive enhancement — degrades gracefully in browsers that don't support it.

font-smoothing.$description: Antialiased rendering on macOS/iOS for sharper serif text. Standard subpixel rendering on other platforms.

scene-break.$description: The pause between scenes within a chapter. The typographic fermata — hold here. More present than whitespace, less ornamental than a motif divider. Implemented as a swelled rule: a horizontal mark that thickens at center and tapers to nothing at the edges. The swelled rule appears in European printing from the 16th century forward; in Indian bookmaking the same function is served by small lotus ornaments or centered dots. The AoY uses a similar device between scenes.
scene-break.implementation: Linear gradient: gold fading to transparent at both edges. Pure CSS, no SVG dependency. Degrades to nothing in text-only mode (whitespace handles the pause).
scene-break.publication-context: Shifts to crimson — the book's voice claiming its scene boundaries.
scene-break.alternative: The motif system's .motif-breath offers a botanical scene break (small lotus at ambient opacity). Choose swelled rule for typographic restraint; choose motif-breath for ornamental warmth.
scene-break.hierarchy: Chapter break > Section divider (motif-divider) > Scene break (reader-scene-break) > Breath (motif-breath) > Whitespace

text-formatting.$description: How different content types are formatted. These are recipes for text treatment.

citation.example: — Paramahansa Yogananda, Autobiography of a Yogi, Chapter 12

epigraph.decoration: Large decorative opening quotation mark (“) in gold at decorative opacity (0.4), positioned absolute top-left
epigraph.padding: generous vertical, extra horizontal for the quotation mark

chapter-heading.treatment: Small caps label above ('CHAPTER XII'), followed by chapter title in display size
chapter-heading.spacing: vast top margin (page-level separation), expansive bottom margin before first paragraph

verse.$description: Poetry and verse quotations from the teachings. Preserve line breaks. Center-aligned with generous surrounding whitespace. Sacred register content (the words are the guru's).
verse.whitespace: generous vertical margins, generous horizontal padding
verse.line-breaks: preserved as authored via white-space: pre-line — never reflowed

font-loading.$description: Font loading strategy. Self-hosted for GDPR compliance (ADR-099). Zero requests to external font services.
font-loading.strategy: font-display: swap — show system font immediately, swap to custom font when loaded
font-loading.subset: Latin + Latin Extended unicode ranges for Merriweather, Lora, Open Sans. Devanagari subset loaded only when Hindi content detected.

text-only-mode.$description: ADR-006 §1. Text-only mode strips all images, SVGs, decorative elements, and custom fonts. Georgia + Times New Roman replace all fonts. Background images removed. Essential for low-bandwidth connections (PRI-05).
text-only-mode.effect: All img, svg:not([role='img']), [data-decorative] hidden. All fonts → Georgia, Times New Roman, serif. All background-image → none.

reader-modes.$description: Named reading modes that alter typography, chrome visibility, and interaction. Each mode is activated via data-mode attribute on the reader container. The modes form a spectrum from full-chrome (normal) to zero-chrome (quiet). Source: DES-006, DES-049.

normal.chrome: Full — header, footer, nav, Related Teachings panel visible
normal.interaction: All features available — dwell, keyboard, golden thread, bookmarks

focus.chrome: Hidden except reading column + next chapter nav
focus.interaction: Keyboard shortcuts, dwell, bookmarks. No golden thread, no Related Teachings.
focus.calm-technology: The seeker chose to focus — honor that choice completely. Simplified reading mode suppresses all peripheral UI.

present.chrome: Hidden — reading column only, no navigation
present.interaction: Arrow-key navigation between passages. Swipe on mobile. No dwell, no bookmarks.
present.purpose: Group reading / digital lectern. Large text fills the screen for shared reading. Landscape preferred.

quiet.chrome: Hidden. Article dimmed (0.05 opacity). Fixed overlay takes over.
quiet.interaction: Timer controls only. No reading interaction.
quiet.purpose: Contextual Quiet Corner. A space for reflection outside the reading flow.

reader-modes.design-principle: Modes are a spectrum of attention: normal (peripheral awareness), focus (content-only), present (shared attention), quiet (inner attention). Each removes UI in service of a different kind of engagement.

bidi-readiness.$description: The CSS layer uses CSS logical properties exclusively — zero physical directional properties (margin-left, padding-right, left, right, float: left, border-left, etc.). This means every reading surface, pattern, and typographic feature flips automatically when dir='rtl' is set on the document or a containing element. PRI-06 requires this from Milestone 2a. Yogananda's teachings exist in Arabic and Urdu translations; YSS has seekers in regions where these scripts are used.

css-mechanism.$description: CSS logical properties map physical directions to the writing mode. inline-start becomes 'left' in LTR and 'right' in RTL. The entire CSS layer — foundations, themes, typography, calm, attention, reading surface patterns — uses logical properties. No CSS changes are needed to support RTL layout.
css-mechanism.validation: Zero instances of margin-left, margin-right, padding-left, padding-right, left:, right:, float: left, float: right, border-left, or border-right in the CSS layer. Enforced by regression test.

what-flips: Drop capital floats to inline-start (left in LTR, right in RTL)
Epigraph decorative quotation mark positions to inline-start
Golden thread accent bar appears on inline-start margin
Footnote backlink padding adjusts to inline-start
Reading surface margins, padding, and indentation
what-does-not-flip: Centered content (text-align: center) — symmetrical, no directional dependency
Block-direction spacing (margin-block, padding-block) — vertical, writing-mode independent
Scene break gradient (linear-gradient to right) — decorative, horizontal direction is intentional
bidi-readiness.activation: Set dir='rtl' on <html> or on a containing element. Combined with lang attribute for proper text shaping: <html lang='ar' dir='rtl'>.

remaining-work.$description: Layout is RTL-ready. What remains for full RTL language support is content-layer work, not design-system work.
remaining-work.typography-stack: No Arabic/Urdu font stack defined yet. When an RTL surface is built, add a locale token file (like foundations/locale/hi.tokens.json) with appropriate font families, line-height adjustments, and reading measure for Arabic/Nastaliq script.
remaining-work.scene-break-direction: The swelled rule uses 'linear-gradient(to right, ...)'. This is intentionally non-flipping — the gradient direction is decorative, not semantic. Revisit if RTL readers find it visually incongruent.
remaining-work.drop-cap-script: Drop capitals are Latin-only (ADR-080). Arabic calligraphic tradition has different opening conventions that should be designed specifically, not adapted from the Latin drop cap.
